questions.question: Why does a watcher created inside an async callback cause a memory leak?
options: Async callbacks run in a separate thread that Vue cannot track
Vue only auto-cleans watchers created synchronously during `setup()`
Watchers inside callbacks always watch too many reactive dependencies

questions.question: What does `watch()` return that allows manual cleanup of a watcher?
options: A Promise that resolves when the watcher stops
A stop function that can be called to stop the watcher
A `ref` whose value you set to `false` to stop it

questions.question: What is the preferred fix when a watcher must depend on data loaded asynchronously?
options: Create the watcher inside the `then()` callback and accept the leak
Create the watcher synchronously with the async condition checked inside the callback
Use `watchEffect` instead, which handles async creation safely
Delay the async operation until after the watcher is registered
